gates.name: send_latency_p99_ms
gates.note: G-S2/bible#3: Swift->Rust send latency p99 <= 1 ms

gates.name: send_latency_p50_ms
gates.note: G-S2: Swift->Rust send latency p50 <= 100 us

gates.name: rss_growth_bytes
gates.note: G-S2: RSS growth <= 20 MiB (PEAK, at flood end — unchanged contract)

gates.name: retained_heap_after_drain_bytes
gates.note: S2-drain: NET heap still live after backlog fully drained; PASS = transient spike, FAIL = real retention under load

gates.name: failed_sends
gates.note: G-S2: all sends accepted (no mpsc disconnects during flood)

gates.name: send_hitch_proxy
gates.note: G-S2: send p99 < 16 ms (no main-thread frame-drop hitches)

notes: Nominal dispatches: 300000; actual: 299992; p50=0.005ms p99=0.089ms; failed_sends: 8
Actor mpsc backlog depth: not directly observable from caller thread; RSS growth is the proxy gate (bounded channel growth = bounded RSS). Hitch gate uses p99 as proxy for individual send latencies.

S2-drain: peak_net_heap=39918768 B, retained_after_drain=39866908 B, reclaimed_by_drain=51860 B, drain=1.5s (4 samples). Verdict: RETAINED under load — heap NOT reclaimed after drain; genuine unbounded growth, a bounded-channel/backpressure fix is mandatory
